behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this failure is at the watcher↔OS filesystem event boundary (recursive watch behavior). A mock emitter can inject a subdirectory path and pass even if the real watcher is not actually subscribed recursively. MOCK-HIDE: mocking filesystem events can hide real missed-subdirectory events.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temporary directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is local dedupe/time-window logic and can be reproduced by direct method/function calls with a fake clock. No real OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA=no). Rejection-gate: higher levels were rejected because the behavior text is specifically "same file path within a configurable time window" and does not require real watcher/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedupe component directly: submit path A at t0, submit path A again at t0+delta (inside window), assert second request is dropped; submit again after window expiry, assert it is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on OS event duplication patterns. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes usually produce idealized event sequences and can miss debounce/coalescing bugs that only appear with real watcher timing/event bursts.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a single file write known to trigger multiple events on the platform (or append/rename patterns), and assert only one pipeline execution/output for that file path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: delete/recreate directory semantics and watcher re-subscription are OS-level filesystem behaviors. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watchers can trivially emit recreate events and pass even if real watcher handles inode/path replacement incorrectly.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watch path, remove that directory while daemon stays alive, recreate it at the same path, then place a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly cross-module handoff (event intake -> metadata lookup -> queue ordering), not a single pure function in many implementations. Integration-brake checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate for System: behavior text only requires ordering by timestamps, not real OS event anomalies/timeouts; can be validated with in-process module wiring and controlled timestamp fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire watcher-event adapter, metadata provider, and scheduler/queue in-process; inject near-simultaneous arrivals for files A/B/C with controlled creation timestamps, then assert processing order follows timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: partial-write timing and filesystem event timing are OS/filesystem behaviors. MOCK-HIDE: a fake file write often appears atomically complete, hiding readiness/stability bugs (e.g., reading half-written content).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, begin writing a large file in chunks with delays, trigger arrival naturally, assert daemon does not process mid-write, then completes processing only after final chunk/close.
